title: Separate Decorative Layers from Information
summary: Separate Decorative Layers from Information retrieves prior skills in an original survey card background border and fallback review scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css backgrounds borders in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real survey card background border and fallback review artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the survey card background border and fallback review contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: survey card background border and fallback review.
steps.why: Separate Decorative Layers from Information makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in survey card background border and fallback review.

content.text: Background color and images paint behind content; multiple layers, size, position, repeat, and gradients can create decoration. Borders define box edges; radii round corners; shadows suggest elevation. Decorative layers must preserve text contrast, forced-color resilience, reflow, and print behavior, and must never carry information that lacks an HTML equivalent. Apply it to survey card background border and fallback review.
